description:
You are a FinOps analyst who has just been asked to pinpoint the three external IP addresses responsible for the highest egress spend and to prepare firewall artefacts that will let the security team block them.

    A CSV log that represents one hour of raw VPC Flow-log exports has already been placed on the system for you at:
        /home/user/logs/egress_traffic.csv

    1. Parse that file, add-up the total number of bytes that left the VPC for every unique          destination (dst_ip), then sort the results from the largest total to the smallest.
    2. Create a directory that will hold all of your deliverables:
           /home/user/finops_firewall
    3. Inside that directory create a human-readable report named:
           /home/user/finops_firewall/egress_report.txt

       The report MUST have exactly the following format (4 lines, no extra blanks, spaces exactly as shown, newline at the end of every line):

           Top 3 High Egress Destinations (bytes_out):
           1. <ip-address-with-dots> : <total-bytes>
           2. <ip-address-with-dots> : <total-bytes>
           3. <ip-address-with-dots> : <total-bytes>

       • Replace <ip-address-with-dots> with the actual IPs.  
       • Replace <total-bytes> with the computed totals.  
       • Make sure the order is descending (largest first).

    4. Write an iptables-restore compatible policy file named:
           /home/user/finops_firewall/egress_block.rules

       The file MUST contain exactly the 7 lines below, in this order, each terminated by a newline character, and using the three IPs discovered in step 1:

           *filter
           :OUTPUT ACCEPT [0:0]
           -A OUTPUT -d <Top_IP>/32 -j DROP
           -A OUTPUT -d <Second_IP>/32 -j DROP
           -A OUTPUT -d <Third_IP>/32 -j DROP
           COMMIT

       Notes:  
       • Keep the star-line, colon-line and COMMIT line unmodified.  
       • Substitute <Top_IP>, <Second_IP>, <Third_IP> with the exact IPs from the report, in the same descending order.

    5. Finally, produce a “saved” view of those rules as if the command `iptables-save` had been run, and store it at:
           /home/user/finops_firewall/iptables-save.out

       The test harness will only check that this file contains the three “-A OUTPUT …” DROP lines exactly as written in egress_block.rules.

    When you are finished the directory /home/user/finops_firewall should contain precisely these three files:
        egress_report.txt
        egress_block.rules
        iptables-save.out

    Do not move or rename the original CSV.  
    Ensure all three new files are world-readable (chmod 644 is sufficient).  
    Console output is welcome but will not be graded; only the presence and exact contents of the three files above will be evaluated.
truth:
Initial file that already exists before the agent starts:

    • Path: /home/user/logs/egress_traffic.csv  
      Contents (11 lines, Unix LF line endings):

        timestamp,src_ip,dst_ip,bytes_out
        2024-05-01T10:00:00Z,10.0.0.5,34.102.120.1,2048
        2024-05-01T10:01:00Z,10.0.0.5,44.12.31.5,10240
        2024-05-01T10:02:00Z,10.0.0.20,34.102.120.1,4096
        2024-05-01T10:03:00Z,10.0.0.20,172.217.3.110,5120
        2024-05-01T10:04:00Z,10.0.0.7,44.12.31.5,8192
        2024-05-01T10:05:00Z,10.0.0.7,34.102.120.1,1024
        2024-05-01T10:06:00Z,10.0.0.7,172.217.3.110,10000
        2024-05-01T10:07:00Z,10.0.0.5,34.102.120.1,3072
        2024-05-01T10:08:00Z,10.0.0.20,44.12.31.5,2048
        2024-05-01T10:09:00Z,10.0.0.5,34.102.120.1,5120

    Correct byte totals per destination:
        44.12.31.5     : 20480
        34.102.120.1   : 15360
        172.217.3.110  : 15120

    Therefore the files the agent must create should contain:

    1. /home/user/finops_firewall/egress_report.txt  
       (exact content, 4 lines):

           Top 3 High Egress Destinations (bytes_out):
           1. 44.12.31.5 : 20480
           2. 34.102.120.1 : 15360
           3. 172.217.3.110 : 15120

    2. /home/user/finops_firewall/egress_block.rules  
       (exact content, 7 lines):

           *filter
           :OUTPUT ACCEPT [0:0]
           -A OUTPUT -d 44.12.31.5/32 -j DROP
           -A OUTPUT -d 34.102.120.1/32 -j DROP
           -A OUTPUT -d 172.217.3.110/32 -j DROP
           COMMIT

    3. /home/user/finops_firewall/iptables-save.out must at minimum include the three lines:

           -A OUTPUT -d 44.12.31.5/32 -j DROP
           -A OUTPUT -d 34.102.120.1/32 -j DROP
           -A OUTPUT -d 172.217.3.110/32 -j DROP

       (Additional header/footer lines are allowed.)

    Permissions: all three newly created files must be 644 or more relaxed.  
    The directory /home/user/finops_firewall must exist and be accessible (755 is fine).
